business_justification: ESXi host runs 12 production VMs including customer-data-api and payment-processor. Patch requires full host evacuation which requires 4-hour maintenance window. vMotion capacity is insufficient for live migration of all 12 VMs simultaneously. Planned host evacuation during December maintenance window was cancelled due to Black Friday/Cyber Monday freeze. Next available window: January 14, 2026.
compensating_controls: 1. WAF with virtual patching rules deployed for all web-facing VMs on this host (Imperva rule set updated Sep 12). 2. Network micro-segmentation — ESXi management interface restricted to dedicated management VLAN (10.30.100.0/24) with ACL allowing only jump-box-01 and vcenter-01. 3. Enhanced monitoring — CrowdStrike Falcon sensors on all guest VMs with behavioral detection. 4. VMware Tools guest isolation enabled — VM escape mitigations active. 5. IDS/IPS signatures for CVE-2025-22224 exploit patterns deployed on fw-perimeter-01 (Palo Alto Threat ID 95823).
review_notes: Q4 revalidation completed December 12, 2025 by Security Operations. All compensating controls verified operational: WAF rules active (last log Dec 12), management VLAN ACLs confirmed (fw audit Dec 10), CrowdStrike sensors reporting (all 12 VMs checked), IDS signatures active (0 hits since deployment). Remediation planned for January 14 maintenance window.

business_justification: Legacy CRM application built on Struts 2.x framework. Patching Struts requires full application rewrite due to framework-level changes. Application retirement planned for March 2026 as part of CRM migration to Salesforce.
compensating_controls: 1. WAF rules blocking known Struts exploitation patterns (ModSecurity OWASP CRS). 2. Application restricted to internal network only — not internet-facing. 3. Input validation layer added at reverse proxy. 4. Application user runs with minimal database privileges (read-only on sensitive tables).
review_notes: Q3 revalidation completed October 25. All controls verified. CRM migration project on track for March 2026.

business_justification: D-Link NAS device used for non-critical archive storage. Vendor has not released a patch. Device replacement approved in Q1 2026 hardware refresh budget.
compensating_controls: 1. Device isolated on dedicated VLAN with no internet access. 2. Access restricted to 3 named users via firewall ACL. 3. No sensitive or regulated data stored on device. 4. Daily backup to S3 archive.
review_notes: Q4 revalidation completed November 18. Device remains isolated. Replacement Synology NAS ordered — delivery expected January 2026.

business_justification: Internal file sharing tool used by 8 developers for non-sensitive build artifacts. Migration to Artifactory planned for Q2 2026.
compensating_controls: 1. Accessible only via VPN — not internet-facing. 2. Single-tenant — only dev team has accounts. 3. No sensitive data — build logs and non-production artifacts only. 4. Network ACL restricts access to engineering VLAN.
review_notes: Q3 revalidation completed October 5. All controls verified. Migration to Artifactory in planning phase.
